methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the official CIS Artificial Intelligence page, the CIS Technion AI tools page, and library AI guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:technion.ac.il Technion artificial intelligence academic integrity plagiarism AI tools academic plagiarism
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced an official Technion Library page on avoiding plagiarism and academic fraud when using AI tools.
The CIS robots.txt allows these service pages; only WordPress admin, plugin, include, and event-calendar paths are disallowed.
The library robots.txt disallows wp-admin and wp-includes for general crawlers; the selected public guidance pages are not disallowed.
Direct HTTP fetches verified official Technion pages and produced normalized text hashes for snapshots.

artifacts.summary: Official Technion discovery found a central CIS Artificial Intelligence guidance page for the whole Technion community, a CIS page describing institutional Copilot and ChatGPT Education licenses, and a Technion Library page on AI, plagiarism, and academic fraud. Course-specific and general tool-list sources were rejected for direct university-wide policy claims.

artifacts.title: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Technion has official public CIS guidance for responsible AI use across the Technion community, including organizational-tool preference, personal/confidential information restrictions, output validation, and CIS approval for new AI tools. CIS also publishes an official Technion AI tools page for Microsoft 365 Copilot and ChatGPT Education licenses. Technion Library publishes academic guidance on AI, plagiarism, source verification, and tool/legal/ethical requirements. A general library AI-tools guide and a course-specific MBA document were not used for direct policy claims because they were too broad or too course-specific.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
CIS guidance claims are scoped to the official CIS pages and their stated target audience.
Library academic-integrity claims are guidance from Technion Library and are not broadened into a disciplinary code unless the source text itself says so.
Course-specific AI permission text discovered in an MBA document was rejected for university-wide claim extraction.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
